name: Yelso de Hayas Menhir Loop from Laredo
name_original: Menhir Yelso de Hayas por el Alto de Guriezo – circular desde Laredo por la costa
description: This difficult 20.7-mile mountain biking route from Laredo offers coastal views, challenging terrain, and the ancient Menhir Yelso de Hayas.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Laredo, Texas, offers diverse outdoor experiences along the Rio Grande. The region features varied landscapes, including dedicated singletrack trails, paths alongside lakes, and routes through creek areas. Terrain ranges from mostly flat to intermediate with uneven sections and small hills, catering to different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Laredo The most popular mountain bike trail is Yelso de Hayas Menhir Loop from Laredo, a 20.7 miles (33.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Menhir Yelso de Hayas – Puebla Vieja de Laredo loop from Laredo, a moderate 20.9 miles (33.7 km) path. This route includes varied terrain and climbs. Local mountain bikers also love the Alto de Guriezo – Menhir Yelso de Hayas loop from Laredo, a 18.9 miles (30.4 km) trail leading through hilly landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. Mountain biking around Laredo is defined by varied terrain, including singletrack, lakeside paths, and creek trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy, flat routes to more challenging paths with elevation changes. The routes in Laredo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Laredo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Laredo?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails around Laredo, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy, mostly flat paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation changes.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on Laredo's mountain bike trails?
A: Laredo's mountain bike trails feature diverse terrain, including dedicated singletrack, paths alongside lakes, and routes through creek areas. You can expect a mix of mostly flat sections and intermediate routes with uneven terrain and small hills.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Laredo?
A: Yes, Laredo offers several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners. Out of the 16 routes listed, 3 are classified as easy, providing a gentle introduction to mountain biking in the area. These typically feature mostly flat paths.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Laredo?
A: The mountain bike trails around Laredo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails in Laredo that form a loop?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes around Laredo are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999671404/yelso-de-hayas-menhir-loop-from-laredo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yelso de Hayas Menhir Loop from Laredo&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 33.3 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10509123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menhir Yelso de Hayas – Puebla Vieja de Laredo loop from Laredo&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 33.7 km ride.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks near the mountain bike trails?
A: Near the mountain bike trails, you can explore several natural attractions. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1834895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Eye&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5389027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marshes Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2422215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Regatón Stream&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features various mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3387497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brusco (Mijedo)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3283461" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miravalles Peak&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find mountain biking trails that offer views of the Rio Grande?
A: The Chacon Creek Hike and Bike Trail is known for its scenic views, with a portion running near the US/Mexico border, offering glimpses of the Rio Grande. Additionally, areas like Riverbend and Las Palmas Nature Trail provide ecological significance along the river.

Q: Are there any state parks in Laredo with mountain biking opportunities?
A: Yes, &lt;strong&gt;Lake Casa Blanca International State Park&lt;/strong&gt; is a central outdoor hub in Laredo that features trails specifically for mountain biking and hiking. It allows riders to explore varied landscapes alongside the lake. For more information on the park, you can visit &lt;a href="https://tpwd.texas.gov/state-parks/lake-casa-blanca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;texas.gov&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Laredo?
A: Laredo's climate means that mountain biking is generally enjoyable outside of the peak summer months when temperatures can be very high. Spring and fall offer more moderate weather conditions, making them ideal for exploring the trails. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Laredo offers challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers. Four of the 16 routes are classified as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains and varied terrain. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31868204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marshes Viewpoint – Playa de Sonabia loop from Laredo&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 53 km long.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking trails in Laredo?
A: Parking is typically available at popular mountain biking locations such as Lake Casa Blanca State Park, La Bota MTB Trails, and Manadas Creek Shiloh Trails. It's advisable to check specific trailheads or park websites for detailed parking information before your visit.

Q: What are the longest mountain bike trails around Laredo?
A: For those seeking longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40323465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Laredo Beach – View of Val Helguera loop from Laredo&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route spanning over 106 km, typically taking more than 9 hours to complete. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31868204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marshes Viewpoint – Playa de Sonabia loop from Laredo&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 53 km.
tips: In the area there are many deposits of gypsum and minerals that were exploited for a long time. In this area, the gypsum mineral veins are found in the cliff and had to be loaded vertically with the help of the tower that is still standing. It is a site visited for being one of the panoramic points with the best views in the area.
Also known as "high cairn", the menhir is approximately 3 meters high and is made of sandstone. It is estimated that it was buried by the inhabitants of the area more than 5,000 years ago.
The views of Laredo beach from this point of the walk are unique. It is a stony beach under the cliffs of Alto de las Minas or Ahorcado, facing the Cantabrian Sea.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Menhir Yelso de Hayas mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a hard mountain bike ride, recommended for riders with good fitness and advanced riding skills. You should be prepared for technical sections with steep ascents and descents, loose gravel, and rocky areas. Some parts may even require dismounting and pushing your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse surface, including approximately 20.8 km of unpaved tracks, 8.73 km of paved tracks, 3.35 km of asphalt, 1.40 km of narrow trails (singletrack), and 282 meters of natural paths. You'll ride through areas with eucalyptus and pine trees, and encounter challenging sections with loose gravel and rocks.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in scenery and historical significance. Key highlights include the ancient Menhir Yelso de Hayas, a 3-meter tall megalithic monument. You'll also pass by the Gypsum Loader and enjoy impressive coastal panoramas, such as the View of Laredo Beach, as well as views of the Cantabrian mountains and Guriezo's natural landscapes.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route, meaning you will end up back at your starting point in Laredo.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Menhir Yelso de Hayas mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Laredo, you can look for public parking options within Laredo. Specific parking areas near the coast or the town center would be suitable starting points for this circular trail.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: To enjoy the diverse scenery and challenging terrain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures. Summer can be quite warm, especially on exposed sections, while winter might bring colder weather and potentially wet, muddy trails, increasing the difficulty.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that overlap with the Northern Route (Cantabria), Senda del Litoral, Ruta de Carlos V, Camino de Liendo, Camino del Campillo, Calle Las Nuevas, Calle Escultor José Gragera, and Paseo Marítimo.

faqs.question: Is the Menhir Yelso de Hayas mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on many trails in the region, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. Given the technical difficulty of this particular mountain bike route, consider if it's suitable for your dog's fitness and ability to navigate challenging terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail or the Parque Natural de las Marismas de Santoña, Victoria y Joyel?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required to access this mountain bike route or to ride within the Parque Natural de las Marismas de Santoña, Victoria y Joyel. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected natural areas, and stay on marked trails.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length (over 33 km), it's crucial to be well-prepared. Bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate protective gear (helmet, gloves), and navigation tools. Weather-appropriate clothing is also essential, as conditions can change.

faqs.question: What natural features or wildlife might I encounter along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses a rich and varied landscape, including verdant forests, meadows, and wetlands characteristic of the Guriezo area and the wider Cantabrian region. You'll experience coastal views and inland mountainous terrain. While specific wildlife sightings are not guaranteed, the diverse habitats mean you might spot various bird species, small mammals, and local flora.
